reset_tests.description: Assert and deassert synchronous active-high rst while holding data_valid=1 and a fixed data pattern such as 12'hA5C; verify enc_valid remains equal to data_valid and enc_codeword remains {^data, data}. This targets mutants that incorrectly insert rst into functional logic.
Toggle rst with data_valid=0 and arbitrary data; verify enc_valid stays 0 and do not check enc_codeword. This confirms reset does not create false valid pulses or forced codewords.

With data and data_valid held constant, toggle clk across multiple edges while rst stays 0; verify outputs do not change in response to clk edges. This targets accidental posedge/negedge registration or clk gating.

normal_tests.description: Exhaustive valid-data sweep: for every 12-bit data value from 0x000 to 0xFFF with data_valid=1, verify enc_valid=1 and enc_codeword={^data, data}. This is the primary oracle and will catch wrong parity polarity, wrong subset width, bit reordering, and wrong parity-bit placement.

Validity gating check: drive representative data samples with data_valid=0 and verify enc_valid=0 only, without checking enc_codeword. Include transitions from valid to invalid and invalid to valid to confirm no stale or delayed valid behavior.

vectors.expect_same_cycle_behavior: enc_valid follows immediately; enc_codeword becomes {^data, data} without extra clock

normal_tests.description: Representative direct-formula spot checks to make the mapping obvious for later oracle generation.

corner_case_tests.description: Boundary and structured patterns: all-zeros, all-ones, alternating bits, inverse alternating bits, single-hot, and single-cold patterns. These strongly discriminate wrong XOR polarity, incorrect bit slicing, and reversed/shifted data placement.
Parity-subset discriminator set: choose vectors where inclusion or exclusion of a specific bit flips parity. These catch mutants using 11 bits, 13 bits, or an incorrect subset.

vectors.rationale: Only bit 11 set; parity must include MSB data bit
Only bit 0 set; parity must include LSB data bit
Two distant bits set; parity must be 0

corner_case_tests.description: Parity-bit-position discriminator set: use vectors where parity differs from both data[11] and data[0] so MSB-vs-LSB append bugs are obvious.

vectors.rationale: Correct output has parity in bit 12; appending parity at LSB would produce a different 13-bit word
Separates prepended parity from accidental data shift or overwrite

corner_case_tests.description: Same-cycle response edge cases: change data and data_valid near a sampling event in the testbench and check outputs after a small combinational settle delay in the same cycle, not one clock later. This separates pure combinational logic from registered-output bugs.

vectors.expect_same_cycle_behavior: enc_valid rises immediately and enc_codeword becomes {^12'hABC,12'hABC}

sequence_tests.description: Multi-cycle independence sequence: across successive cycles, vary clk and rst while applying a deterministic stream of data/data_valid values. Expected outputs at each observation point depend only on current data_valid and current data, with no memory, no buffering, and no cycle latency.
Valid-pulse sequence: issue isolated one-cycle pulses on data_valid with changing data values and verify enc_valid pulses in the same cycle only, with no one-cycle-early or one-cycle-late behavior.

random_tests.description: Generate 200 random trials with uniformly random data[11:0], random data_valid, and independent random clk/rst toggles. For each trial, if data_valid==1 check enc_valid==1 and enc_codeword=={^data,data}; if data_valid==0 check only enc_valid==0. Bias at least 25% of samples toward near-neighbor transitions from the previous data word to expose stale-output and delayed-update bugs, and include occasional rapid clk/rst toggles while inputs are stable to confirm clock/reset independence.

key_discriminators: Primary oracle is exact same-cycle mapping: if data_valid==1, enc_codeword must equal {^data[11:0], data[11:0]} and enc_valid must be 1.
If data_valid==0, only enc_valid==0 is checked; enc_codeword must not be overconstrained.
The parity bit is enc_codeword[12], not enc_codeword[0]; data must appear unchanged in enc_codeword[11:0].
The total 13-bit codeword must always have even parity whenever data_valid==1.
clk and rst are interface-only for this block; toggling either must not alter functional outputs except through changed data/data_valid.
Outputs are combinational, so any one-cycle-late, one-cycle-early, posedge-only, or negedge-only behavior is a bug.
Single-hot and single-cold data patterns discriminate wrong XOR polarity, wrong bit omission, and reversed or shifted data slices.
Transitions on data_valid must be immediate and not leave stale enc_valid or stale enc_codeword from a previous valid cycle.
